$comment: mdk - mission control (single pane). The Monday-demo operator wall: ONE screen that fuses the live OTel golden signals (spend, throughput, error rate, p95 latency, in-flight) from Prometheus with the overnight analyst's structured output (health gauge + plain-English anomalies) from the ADR 047 Observability Intelligence API (now on main). Movate-themed (dashboards/grafana/theme/palette.json). Designed to render with non-empty panels against a freshly `mdk demo seed`-ed tenant+project: the seed runs the analyst (PR #613) under project_id='default', so the health gauge + anomaly table light up, and the Prometheus panels populate from the seeded run/cost/latency telemetry once it is scraped. CONSUMES existing endpoints only -- no new /api/v1 routes. Response shapes are the REAL ones (verified against movate.runtime.schemas.ObservabilityInsightView / ObservabilityHealthView and movate.core.observability.analyst): GET /api/v1/observability/health -> {project_id, date, health_score, narrative_digest, anomaly_count, has_insight} (flat, root_selector=''); GET /api/v1/observability/insights -> {insights:[{date, health_score, anomalies:[{metric, severity, value, baseline, z, note}], top_failures:[{signature, count, sample_message, agent}], usage_rollup:{...}, trends:{...}, narrative_digest}], count} (root_selector='insights', and the plain-English anomaly feed flattens insights.anomalies via root_selector='insights.anomalies').

__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Prometheus data source scraping the OTLP -> Prometheus stream that carries mdk's OTel metrics (same source as the #518 golden-signal dashboards). Feeds the live spend / throughput / error-rate / p95-latency / in-flight panels.

__inputs.name: DS_INSIGHTS
__inputs.label: Insights API (JSON / Infinity)
__inputs.description: JSON/Infinity datasource pointed at the ADR 047 Observability Intelligence API (now on main): GET /api/v1/observability/health + /insights. Feeds the health gauge and the plain-English overnight-anomaly table. Plugin: yesoreyeram-infinity-datasource. See dashboards/grafana/insights/README.md for datasource setup.

description: mdk - mission control (single pane). The one operator screen for the demo: a fleet HEALTH gauge + live SPEND / THROUGHPUT / ERROR-RATE, P95 LATENCY + IN-FLIGHT, and the overnight analyst's TOP ANOMALIES IN PLAIN ENGLISH -- all on one wall. Live golden signals come from the real OTel metrics on Prometheus (mdk.run.cost_usd, mdk.jobs.completed, mdk.job.duration_ms, mdk.jobs.in_flight); the health gauge + anomaly narrative come from the ADR 047 Observability Intelligence API (GET /api/v1/observability/health + /insights, now on main). Movate-themed. Lights up against `mdk demo seed` (the seed runs the analyst under project_id='default'). For the deep-dive SRE view see mdk-golden-signals.json; for the full daily narrative see insights/mdk-daily-insights.json; for the leadership view see mdk-exec-summary.json.

options.content:
# Movate MDK — Mission Control

**The one operator screen.** Fleet **health** (overnight analyst) · live **spend / throughput / error rate** · **p95 latency + in-flight** · the analyst's **top anomalies in plain English**. Golden signals are live from the OTel metrics on Prometheus; the health gauge and the anomaly narrative come from the **ADR 047 Observability Intelligence API** (`GET /api/v1/observability/health` + `/insights`, now on main). Seed a live story with `mdk demo seed` — the seed runs the analyst under project `default`, so the health gauge and the anomaly table light up immediately. Deep-dive + narrative + leadership views: top-right links.

panels.title: Fleet health
panels.description: Composite health_score (0-100) for $insight_project from GET /api/v1/observability/health (ADR 047). The API returns a FLAT object: {project_id, date, health_score, narrative_digest, anomaly_count, has_insight} -- so root_selector is '' (top-level field). The score blends success rate, latency-vs-baseline, cost-vs-baseline, drift and eval pass-rate (movate.core.observability.analyst.compute_health_score). Thresholds (Movate palette): red <50, amber 50-79, green >=80. Lights up after `mdk demo seed` runs the analyst for project='default'.

panels.title: Spend this period
panels.description: Total LLM spend over the dashboard window from mdk.run.cost_usd (the #518 catalog instrument). The headline cost number for the demo wall. Pairs with the error-rate and throughput tiles to the right.

panels.title: Throughput (runs)
panels.description: Total runs over the window from mdk.jobs.completed -- the demo's adoption/throughput headline, with an area sparkline of the rate. Same instrument as the #518 golden-signals throughput panel.

panels.title: Error rate
panels.description: Fleet error rate (all non-success) over the window from mdk.jobs.completed: non-success / all. Thresholds match the #518 error SLO -- green <=10% error, amber to red above. The seeded fleet carries a believable 0.5-4% smear of error/safety/dead-letter so this tile is non-zero but healthy.

panels.title: Job latency p95 / p99
panels.description: Job latency p95 + p99 over time from the mdk.job.duration_ms histogram (the #518 catalog instrument). The red line is the 30s high-latency SLO. The seeded fleet injects a latency regression on one agent after a deploy, so p95 shows a believable step change in the window.

panels.title: In-flight now
panels.description: Jobs currently executing from the mdk.jobs.in_flight up/down counter (the #518 saturation signal) -- the live 'work in the pipe right now' number, with a sparkline of recent depth. NOTE: this is a real-time gauge; on a paused/historical demo (replaying a seeded window) it reads 0 because no jobs are executing live. It animates when the runtime is actively draining work (e.g. `mdk dev` / a load run). Documented so an empty value on a static demo is understood, not mistaken for a misconfiguration.

panels.title: Overnight analyst — top anomalies in plain English (from the seeded insights)
Top anomalies (plain English)
panels.description: The overnight analyst's anomalies, flattened across the insight history for $insight_project, worst-z-first. Source: GET /api/v1/observability/insights -> {insights:[{date, anomalies:[{metric, severity, value, baseline, z, note}], ...}], count}. root_selector='insights.anomalies' flattens the nested per-day anomaly arrays into rows; the human-readable `note` (e.g. 'cost is 12.9 sigma above the 3-day baseline') is the plain-English column. These are produced PURE (z-score, no LLM) by movate.core.observability.analyst.detect_anomalies, so they populate $0/offline right after `mdk demo seed` (the seed injects a cost spike + a latency regression, so the table is non-empty). Severity color map: critical dark-red, warning amber, info blue (Movate palette).

panels.title: Open anomalies (latest insight)
panels.description: anomaly_count from the latest insight for $insight_project, via GET /api/v1/observability/health -> {..., anomaly_count, ...} (flat; root_selector=''). The single 'how many fires right now' number paired with the table to the left. 0 (green) on a quiet day; turns amber/red as the analyst flags more.

panels.title: How to read mission control (+ what lights up on a seed)

options.content:
**One screen, two data planes.**

* **Live golden signals** (Spend, Throughput, Error rate, p95/p99 latency, In-flight) read the real OTel metrics on Prometheus (`mdk.run.cost_usd`, `mdk.jobs.completed`, `mdk.job.duration_ms`, `mdk.jobs.in_flight`) — the #518 catalog. Scope with the `$tenant` variable.
* **Fleet health** + **Top anomalies (plain English)** + **Open anomalies** read the ADR 047 Observability Intelligence API (`GET /api/v1/observability/health` + `/insights`) for `$insight_project`. The anomaly `note` (e.g. _“cost is 12.9 sigma above the 3-day baseline”_) is computed **pure (no LLM)** by the analyst, so it populates **offline and free**.

**Lights up on `mdk demo seed`.** The seed persists a believable fleet **and runs the analyst** under project `default` (PR #613), injecting a **cost spike** + a **latency regression after a deploy**. So right after a seed: the health gauge shows a real composite score, the anomaly table is non-empty (cost / volume / latency rows with plain-English notes), and — once the seeded run/cost/latency telemetry is scraped into Prometheus — the spend / throughput / error-rate / p95 tiles populate too.

**Honest gap.** **In-flight** is a *live* gauge (`mdk.jobs.in_flight`): on a static/replayed demo it reads **0** because nothing is executing at that instant — it animates only while the runtime is actively draining work. The **narrative digest** prose is the analyst's one optional LLM call; on the offline seed path it runs against the MockProvider, so the *plain-English signal you should demo is the anomaly `note` column*, not the digest. See `dashboards/grafana/insights/README.md`.

list.label: Insight project
list.name: insight_project
list.description: The ADR 047 project the analyst writes insights under. `mdk demo seed` analyzes under 'default' (movate.cli.demo_cmd._DEMO_INSIGHT_PROJECT), so the health gauge + anomaly table light up for that value out of the box.

title: mdk - mission control (single pane)
